skill.name: azure-resource-manager-playwright-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright .NET SDK to provision and manage Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces, quotas, and name availability through Azure Resource Manager.
skill.summary: Manage Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces with the Azure Resource Manager .NET SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, weak crypto, and a critical combination heuristic. Review found these are markdown documentation examples and Microsoft documentation links, not executable skill code or credential exfiltration. The remaining low risk is that the skill documents Azure credential environment variables and local authentication settings that users must handle carefully.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Detections in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported command patterns are documentation snippets for installing NuGet packages and using SDK APIs. They are not executable skill code, and no shell wrapper or automation file is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only command-like content is inside fenced markdown or a reference table. No file in scope contains runnable script logic.

low_findings.title: Azure Credential Environment Variables Documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents Azure tenant, client, and client secret variables for service principal authentication. This is legitimate SDK usage, but users must avoid committing secrets or exposing logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable names are explicit and include a client secret. The surrounding guidance uses DefaultAzureCredential and does not send the secret elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Documentation Links and Example URIs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are a placeholder data plane URI and Microsoft Learn documentation links. No evidence found of automatic network calls by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in prose and markdown links only. There is no code path that fetches, posts, or exfiltrates data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography locations, but the referenced lines contain front matter and a markdown table header. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no cryptographic algorithm usage. The detections do not match the semantic content.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined documentation examples for commands, URLs, and Azure environment variables. Manual review found no executable code, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky categories exist only as user-facing SDK guidance. The skill does not include scripts, hidden instructions, or network execution logic.

content.value_statement: Provisioning Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces requires the correct Azure Resource Manager SDK flow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate .NET management-plane guidance for workspaces, quotas, and name checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright and Azure.Identity packages.
Shows how to authenticate ARM clients with DefaultAzureCredential.
Guides creation, retrieval, listing, update, and deletion of Playwright Testing workspaces.
Covers subscription-level and workspace-level quota access patterns.
Distinguishes management-plane SDK usage from test execution SDK usage.
limitations: It does not run Playwright tests or configure browser execution.
It does not replace Azure RBAC, subscription, or tenant setup.
It depends on the installed Azure SDK version matching the documented API.
It provides examples, not a complete production deployment framework.

use_cases.title: Provision Test Workspaces
use_cases.description: Create repeatable .NET guidance for provisioning Microsoft Playwright Testing workspaces in an Azure resource group.

use_cases.title: Audit Workspace Quotas
use_cases.description: Generate SDK guidance for listing subscription and workspace quota data before expanding test capacity.

use_cases.title: Separate Management From Test Runs
use_cases.description: Clarify when to use Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright instead of the NUnit test execution package.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a Microsoft Playwright Testing workspace with Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright in .NET.

prompt_templates.title: Check Name Availability
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me check whether a Playwright Testing workspace name is available before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: List Quotas and Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate .NET guidance to list Playwright Testing workspaces and quota information across my subscription.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Management Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an idempotent Azure Resource Manager workflow for creating, updating, validating, and deleting Playwright Testing workspaces.

output_examples.input: Create a new Playwright Testing workspace in an existing resource group.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step .NET management-plane flow covering authentication, resource group lookup, workspace data setup, creation, and returned workspace details.

output_examples.input: Find the quotas for Playwright Testing in West US 3.
output_examples.output: Guidance for using subscription quota APIs and workspace quota collections, with notes about location selection and result fields.

output_examples.input: Explain which SDK I need for running tests.
output_examples.output: A clear comparison showing that Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright manages workspaces, while the separate Playwright Testing NUnit package runs tests.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential and avoid hardcoded client secrets.
Use completed long-running operations when later steps depend on the workspace.
Store the data plane URI securely for test execution configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not use this management SDK as the browser test runner.
Do not commit Azure client secrets or subscription identifiers.
Do not enable local authentication without reviewing your security policy.

faq.question: Does this skill run Playwright tests?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Azure Resource Manager operations for Playwright Testing workspaces.

faq.question: Which .NET package does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright for management-plane operations.

faq.question: Can it help create a workspace?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for creating and updating workspaces through ARM APIs.

faq.question: Does it require Azure credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples assume Azure Identity credentials with the needed subscription permissions.

faq.question: Can it check workspace name availability?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the name availability request for Playwright workspace resources.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in production?
faq.answer: Use it as implementation guidance and apply your normal Azure security, RBAC, and secret handling controls.
